This weekend, I felt the unforeseen hand of fate work in my favor, in another of its many mysterious ways. A chance meeting with a longtime friend who lives in my neighborhood, who also happens to be the President of the Homeowners' Association within this community, turned into the start of what could potentially be a long term partnership. What I had initially anticipated to be a leisurely set of rounds walking around the cul de sac turned into a surprisingly exciting workout session, as she sought to instill in me the work ethic of a champion, impressing upon me many of the techniques used by world champions in various sports--including boxing and football--to stay in top physical shape and condition themselves for the grueling amount of exertion it takes to prevail in an athletic contest. Today, I am due to meet with her again after work for another few rounds of exercise. I feel a slight soreness in my body, but also a great sense of satisfaction; as the old saying goes, "if you don't feel it, it's not working." In my daily experiences I am surrounded by people who inspire me very much with their dedication and focus, who have evidently put in the 10,000 hours it takes to become an expert at what they do. Seeing them in their element, living their best lives, having fulfilled their potential and mastered their trades, fuels a fire that burns in me to strive for greatness. As of only a few weeks ago at the time of writing this, I was creating letters at a nearly mechanical pace, with the intent of sending them to a diversity of individuals in hopes of compelling them to make time out of their busy schedules to visit the Boca Raton Achievement Center. Now, I have put that hobby on hold to keep my mind focused on the task in front of me. I realize that all of these people I had been writing to, all of whom are known for having aspired to the highest possible stations in life, for reaching the pinnacle of a particular profession, made it there not by fluke but through diligence, concentration, and dedication. I remember mentioning to Evelyn in the car that the person who has inspired me the most, not counting my own parents and others who have personally helped me over the years, and not counting Jesus Christ, is the great Rocky Marciano. Rocky's place in sports is quite unique as he became the only heavyweight champion in boxing history to retire with an unbeaten and untied professional record, and successfully stayed retired. Rocky won all 49 of his pro contests, went 7-0 in title bouts and achieved the highest knockout percentage of any heavyweight champion at 87.75%, which becomes even more impressive when you realize Rocky was only 5'9" and had a 68" reach, the shortest of any heavyweight champion. He made up for what he lacked in stature with an insanely intensive training regimen. He would routinely deny interviews to the media, and went for months at a time without seeing his family (including missing birthdays and holidays), isolating himself as much as possible from the outside world in order to devote himself fully to his athletic endeavors. Marciano willed himself to greatness, and in his life story I'm able to find parallels with my own, including the fact that both of us contracted pneumonia when we were young and almost died of it. Perhaps it is yet another dream destined for the backburner, but it's also another goal to strive for. Sometimes, while I am asleep, I feel thoughts come to me. To me, knowing you are not perfect but still embracing your imperfections rather than being ashamed of them is truly a superpower.
